summary: type-context ships fetch-and-eval RCE disguised as a pino logger middleware
details: The package presents itself as a pino-style logger, but its exported middleware in index.js spawns lib/caller.js as a detached child process that fetches JavaScript from https://jsonhosting.com/api/json/fb84ebf4/raw and executes the response via `new Function.constructor("require", s)(require)`, giving the remote host arbitrary code execution in the consumer's Node process with the package's `require` available. A secondary endpoint is stored base64-encoded in lib/const.js under a `DEV_API_KEY` label and decodes to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3. The middleware performs no logging work and only serves to trigger the remote loader before calling next(), consistent with an impersonation lure for the RCE surface. The fetch target is a mutable third-party JSON-hosting service unrelated to the declared logger purpose, and content served there can be changed at any time to deliver arbitrary payloads.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
